samples.target_compile_errors:
error: usage of an `unsafe` block
  --> benchmark_results/2026-05-06T18-03-03/gpt-4o-mini/declarations/type_def_1/sample_001/target.rs:32:5
   |
32 |     unsafe { G_STORE }
   |     ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
   |
   = note: requested on the command line with `-F unsafe-code`

error[E0277]: `main` has invalid return type `i32`
  --> benchmark_results/2026-05-06T18-03-03/gpt-4o-mini/declarations/type_def_1/sample_001/target.rs:35:14
   |
35 | fn main() -> i32 {
   |              ^^^ `main` can only return types that implement `Termination`
   |
   = help: consider using `()`, or a `Result`

warning: variable does not need to be mutable
  --> benchmark_results/2026-05-06T18-03-03/gpt-4o-mini/declarations/type_def_1/sample_001/target.rs:36:9
   |
36 |     let mut distance: MILES = 40;
   |         ----^^^^^^^^
   |         |
   |         help: remove this `mut`
   |
   = note: `#[warn(unused_mut)]` (part of `#[warn(unused)]`) on by default

error[E0507]: cannot move out of `*zp` which is behind a shared reference
  --> benchmark_results/2026-05-06T18-03-03/gpt-4o-mini/declarations/type_def_1/sample_001/target.rs:79:20
   |
79 |         let copy = *zp;
   |                    ^^^ move occurs because `*zp` has type `Range`, which does not implement the `Copy` trait
   |
note: if `Range` implemented `Clone`, you could clone the value
  --> benchmark_results/2026-05-06T18-03-03/gpt-4o-mini/declarations/type_def_1/sample_001/target.rs:4:1
   |
 4 | struct Range {
   | ^^^^^^^^^^^^ consider implementing `Clone` for this type
...
79 |         let copy = *zp;
   |                    --- you could clone this value
help: consider removing the dereference here
   |
79 -         let copy = *zp;
79 +         let copy = zp;
   |
